channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Famitsu Sales: 3/23/26 – 3/29/26
item.content:encoded:
Famitsu has published its estimated physical game software data for Japan for the week of March 16, 2026 to March 22, 2026.

Pokemon Pokopia for Switch 2 remained the highest selling game of the week, moving an additional 48,772 retail copies.

Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection was the highest selling new release of the week, opening at 18,238 retail copies sold on Switch. The PlayStation 5 version did not chart in the top 10.

Super Mario Bros. Wonder – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park debuted at 10,158 retail copies sold.

On the hardware side, Switch 2 sold 65,725 units, the PlayStation 5 family sold 19,404 units, the Switch family sold 18,542 units, the Xbox Series family sold 440 units, and the PlayStation 4 family sold 16 units.

Get the full sales charts below.

Software Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. [SW2] Pokemon Pokopia (The Pokemon Company, 03/05/26) – 48,772 (821,687)
  2. [NSW] Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection (Capcom, 03/27/26) – 18,238 (New)
  3. [SW2] Super Mario Bros. Wonder – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park (Nintendo, 03/26/26) – 10,158 (New)
  4. [SW2] Mario Kart World (Nintendo, 06/05/25) – 7,005 (2,880,343)
  5. [SW2] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (Capcom, 03/13/26) – 5,477 (53,489)
  6. [PS5] Crimson Desert (Pearl Abyss, 03/19/26) – 5,265 (35,373)
  7. [NSW] Pokemon FireRed Version / LeafGreen Version (Download Card) (The Pokemon Company, 02/28/26) – 5,214 (11,146)
  8. [NSW] Winning Post 10 2026 (Koei Tecmo, 03/26/26) – 4,747 (New)
  9. [SW2] Mario Tennis Fever (Nintendo, 02/12/26) – 4,657 (95,632)
  10. [PS5] Winning Post 10 2026 (Koei Tecmo, 03/26/26) – 4,540 (New)

Rankings 11 to 30 will be announced on April 3.

  1. [PS5] Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection (Capcom, 03/27/26) – (New)
  2. [PS5] Life is Strange: Reunion (Square Enix, 03/26/26) – (New)
  3. [SW2] Winning Post 10 2026 (Koei Tecmo, 03/26/26) – (New)
  4. [NSW] Etrange Overlord (Broccoli, 03/26/26) – (New)
  5. [PS5] Curse Warrior (Nippon Ichi Software, 03/26/26) – (New)
  6. [PS4] Curse Warrior (Nippon Ichi Software, 03/26/26) – (New)
  7. [NSW] Curse Warrior (Nippon Ichi Software, 03/26/26) – (New)
  8. [NSW] Okayu Nyumu! R (Entergram, 03/26/26) – (New)
  9. [PS5] Screamer (Milestone, 03/26/26) – (New)
  10. [NSW] TAITO Milestones 4 (TAITO, 03/26/26) – (New)
  11. [PS5] Until Then (Beep Japan, 03/26/26) – (New)
  12. [NSW] Until Then (Beep Japan, 03/26/26) – (New)
  13. [PS5] Resident Evil Requiem (Capcom, 02/27/26) –
  14. [NSW] Animal Crossing: New Horizons (Nintendo, 03/20/20) –
  15. [NSW] Minecraft (Microsoft, 06/21/18) –
  16. [SW2] Animal Crossing: New Horizons – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 01/15/26) –
  17. [SW2] Pokemon Legends: Z-A – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  18. [NSW] Pokemon Legends: Z-A (The Pokemon Company, 10/16/25) –
  19. [NSW] Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (Nintendo, 12/07/18) –
  20. [NSW] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  21. [SW2] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  22. [NSW] Nintendo Switch Sports (Nintendo, 04/29/22) –
  23. [SW2] Kirby Air Riders (Nintendo, 11/20/25) –
  24. [PS5] Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection (Capcom, 03/13/26) –
  25. [NSW] Mario Kart 8 Deluxe (Nintendo, 04/28/17) –
  26. [SW2] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  27. [SW2] Super Mario Party Jamboree – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Jamboree TV (Nintendo, 07/24/25) –
  28. [SW2] Donkey Kong Bananza (Nintendo, 07/17/25) –
  29. [NSW] Super Mario Party Jamboree (Nintendo, 10/17/24) –
  30. [PS5] MLB The Show 26 (SIE, 03/17/26) –
  31. [NSW] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  32. [PS5] Dragon Quest VII Reimagined (Square Enix, 02/05/26) –
  33. [NSW] Splatoon 3 (Nintendo, 09/09/22) –
  34. [NSW] Clubhouse Games: 51 Worldwide Classics (Nintendo, 06/05/20) –
  35. [NSW] Pokemon Scarlet / Violet (The Pokemon Company, 11/18/22) –
  36. [PS5] Tokyo Xtreme Racer (Genki, 02/26/26) –

Hardware Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. Switch 2 – 65,725 (4,951,516)
  2. PlayStation 5 Digital Edition – 11,941 (1,236,374)
  3. Switch OLED Model – 8,722 (9,518,861)
  4. Switch – 4,963 (20,266,978)
  5. Switch Lite – 4,857 (6,899,157)
  6. PlayStation 5 Pro – 4,662 (340,708)
  7. PlayStation 5 – 2,801 (5,909,916)
  8. Xbox Series X Digital Edition – 353 (29,084)
  9. Xbox Series S – 61 (341,493)
  10. Xbox Series X – 26 (325,427)
  11. PlayStation 4 – 16 (7,930,426)

item.title: Hunter x Hunter: Nen x Impact DLC character Shizuku launches April 16
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch

Member #8 of the Phantom Troupe.

Hunter x Hunter: Nen x Impact downloadable content character Shizuku will launch on April 16, publisher Bushiroad Games and developer Eighting announced.

Hunter x Hunter: Nen x Impact is available now for PlayStation 5, Switch, and PC via Steam.

Watch a new trailer below.

DLC Character Shizuku Trailer

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/hunter-x-hunter-nen-x-impact-dlc-character-shizuku-launches-april-16
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Jaleco Sports: Goal! 2 announced for PS5, Switch; now available
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Rock It Games and developer Sickhead Games have announced Jaleco Sports: Goal! 2 for PlayStation 5 and Switch. It is available now for $7.99.

Here is an overview of the game, via Rock It Games:

Jaleco Sports: Goal! 2 includes four games—three from the Nintendo Entertainment System / Famicom era: Goal! Two (United States / Nintendo Entertainment System, 1989), Goal! 2 (Europe / Nintendo Entertainment System, 1989), and Goal! (Japan / Famicom, 1989)—and one title from the Super Nintendo, Super Goal! 2 (US-SNES – 1992).

Goal! Two shifted the game perspective from the top-down view of the original Goal! to an angled overhead view with a scrolling screen.

An additional eight teams have been added to Goal! Two (total of 24) and overall team roster size is 15 players, with the ability to choose your 11 starters based on their stats.

The Super Nintendo Entertainment System sequel, Super Goal! 2, expanded on the original with a side-scrolling field, 24 international teams, instant replays, and full-field radar views. It also introduced strategic options like four defensive formations and more player control.

Watch a set of trailers below. View a set of screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer (with Jaleco Sports: Bases Loaded II)

Announce Trailer (PlayStation 5)

Announce Trailer (Switch)

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/jaleco-sports-goal-2-announced-for-ps5-switch-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Jaleco Sports: Bases Loaded II announced for PS5, Switch; now available
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Rock It Games and developer Sickhead Games have announced Jaleco Sports: Bases Loaded II for PlayStation 5 and Switch. It is available now for $7.99.

Here is an overview of the game, via Rock It Games:

About

Bases Loaded II: Second Season was originally released for the Nintendo Entertainment System in 1989 and was revolutionary in its introduction of Player Performance Ratings, that were designed to simulate the hot streaks and slumps that every player went through during a baseball season. Batters and pitchers were each rated in three categories: Physical, Sensitivity and Intellectual, and these ratings would rise and fall as the season wore on. The Player Performance Ratings added an extra layer of strategy to the game as managers would have to determine if one of their hitters was in enough of a slump to warrant a few games on the bench.

Super Bases Loaded II was Jaleco’s second baseball title for the Super Nintendo and featured a battery backup for saving statistics for all the 14 teams in two fictional leagues featured in the game, as well as the four “edit” teams. The game was the first baseball title for the SNES to add a DSP-1 chip to provide 3D like camera effects (Mode 7).

Jaleco Sports: Bases Loaded II features five games total including both Bases Loaded II: Second Season (United States / Nintendo Entertainment System, 1989) and Moero!! Pro Yakyuu ’88 Kettei Ban (Japan / Famicom, 1988), as well as three Super Nintendo / Super Famicom titles: Super Bases Loaded II (United States / Super Nintendo Entertainment System, 1994), Super 3D Baseball (Japan / Super Famicom, 1992) and Hanguk Pro Yagu (Korea / Super Comboy, 1994).

Watch a set of trailers below. View a set of screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer (with Jaleco Sports: Goal! 2)

Announce Trailer (PlayStation 5)

Announce Trailer (Switch)

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/jaleco-sports-bases-loaded-ii-announced-for-ps5-switch-now-available
